briefdescription.para: RX buffer request handled too late.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to event structure. Event is allocated on the stack so it is available only within the context of the event handler.
Context passed to the interrupt handler, set on initialization.

briefdescription.para: Function for initializing the UARTE driver.

detaileddescription.para: This function configures UARTE but peripheral is kept disabled to reduce power consumption.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the driver instance structure.
Pointer to the structure with the initial configuration.
Event handler provided by the user. If not provided driver works in blocking mode.
Initialization was successful.
The driver is already initialized.
Invalid configuration.
Some other peripheral with the same instance ID is already in use. This is possible only if Peripheral Resource Sharing (PRS) module is enabled.
Peripheral was not properly deinitialized after use.

briefdescription.para: Function for reconfiguring the UARTE driver.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the driver instance structure.
Pointer to the structure with the configuration.
Reconfiguration was successful.
The driver is during transfer.
The driver is uninitialized.
Peripheral was not properly deinitialized after use.

briefdescription.para: Function for uninitializing the UARTE driver.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the driver instance structure.

briefdescription.para: Function for checking if the UARTE driver instance is initialized.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the driver instance structure.
Instance is already initialized.
Instance is not initialized.

briefdescription.para: Function for getting the address of the specified UARTE task.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the driver instance structure.
Task.

briefdescription.para: Function for getting the address of the specified UARTE event.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the driver instance structure.
Event.

memberdef.argsstring: (nrfx_uarte_t *p_instance, uint8_t const *p_data, size_t length, uint32_t flags)
memberdef.name: nrfx_uarte_tx

briefdescription.para: Function for sending data over UARTE.

detaileddescription.para: If an event handler is provided in nrfx_uarte_init() call, this function returns immediately (unless special flags are used) and the handler is called when the transfer is done. Otherwise, the transfer is performed in blocking mode, that is, this function returns when the transfer is finished.

simplesect.para: Peripherals using EasyDMA (including UARTE) require the transfer buffers to be placed in the Data RAM region. If this condition is not met, this function will attempt to use the cache buffer provided in the configuration and if it is not available it will return error.
To achieve the lowest power consumption, transmitter is stopped and peripheral is disabled (if receiver is not used) when transfer is completed.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the driver instance structure.
Pointer to data.
Number of bytes to send. Maximum possible length is dependent on the used SoC (see the MAXCNT register description in the Product Specification). The driver checks it with assertion.
Option flags. See Flags used for TX..
Initialization was successful.
Driver is busy transferring the data.
The transfer was aborted from a different context (blocking mode only) or transfer cannot be performed due to driver state, configuration or transfer parameters.
p_data does not point to RAM buffer and cache buffer is not provided or attempted to use non DMA buffer with linked transfer (see NRFX_UARTE_TX_LINK).
Flag NRFX_UARTE_TX_EARLY_RETURN is used but length exceeds internal buffer size.
Peripheral was not properly deinitialized after use.

briefdescription.para: Function for checking if UARTE is currently transmitting.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the driver instance structure.
The UARTE is transmitting.
The UARTE is not transmitting.

briefdescription.para: Function for aborting any ongoing transmission.

simplesect.para: When abortion is not synchronous, the NRFX_UARTE_EVT_TX_DONE event will be generated in non-blocking mode. It will contain the number of bytes sent until the abort was called. If NRFX_UARTE_TX_LINK flag was used for the transfer and linked transfer have not started yet, there will be the second NRFX_UARTE_EVT_TX_DONE event with length equal to 0. The event handler will be called from the UARTE interrupt context.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the driver instance structure.
If true operation is synchronous. Transmitter is stopped upon function return and no event is generated.
Successfully initiated abort or when transmitter synchronously stopped.
Attempt to asynchronously abort when no transfer is active.

detaileddescription.para: The event handler will be called from the caller context with the NRFX_UARTE_EVT_RX_BUF_REQUEST event. The user may respond and provide a buffer using nrfx_uarte_rx_buffer_set. An error is returned if buffer is not provided. After that, the receiver is started and another NRFX_UARTE_EVT_RX_BUF_REQUEST is generated. If a new buffer is not provided, then the receiver is disabled once the first buffer becomes full. If a new buffer is provided, then the receiver will seamlessly switch to a new buffer (using a hardware shortcut).

simplesect.para: If transmitter is inactive then peripheral is disabled after receiver is stopped to achieve the lowest power consumption.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the driver instance structure.
Option flags. See Flags used for RX..
Receiver successfully enabled.
When receiver is already enabled.
When buffer was not provided.

detaileddescription.para: The function should be called as a response to the NRFX_UARTE_EVT_RX_BUF_REQUEST event. If the function is called before enabling the receiver, the first buffer is configured. If the function is called and there is no active buffer but the receiver is enabled but not started, it starts reception.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the driver instance structure.
Pointer to a buffer.
Buffer length.
Buffer successfully set.
Buffer provided without pending request.
No cache buffer provided or blocking mode is used, transfer cannot be handled.
Previous buffer is still in use.
Provided uncached buffer after providing cached one.

briefdescription.para: Function for testing the receiver state in blocking mode.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the driver instance structure.
Pointer to the variable to be filled with the number of received bytes. Can be NULL.
The receiving operation is completed.
The receiver did not complete the operation.
The receiver is disabled.
Operation is not supporting in the current configuration.

briefdescription.para: Function for aborting any ongoing reception.

simplesect.para: NRFX_UARTE_EVT_RX_DONE event will be generated in non-blocking mode. It will contain number of bytes received until the abort was called. The event handler will be called from the UARTE interrupt context.

When the double-buffering feature is used and the UARTE interrupt is processed with a delay (for example, due to a higher priority interrupt) long enough for the first buffer to be filled completely, the event handler will be supplied with the pointer to the first buffer and the number of bytes received in the second buffer. This is because from hardware perspective it is impossible to deduce the reception of which buffer has been aborted. To prevent this from happening, keep the UARTE interrupt latency low or use large enough reception buffers.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the driver instance structure.
If true, UARTE is stopped. If false and there is a second RX buffer provided, only the first transfer is stopped.
If true, receiver is disabled synchronously.
Successfully initiate disabling or disabled (synchronous mode).
Receiver was not enabled.

briefdescription.para: Function for reading error source mask.

simplesect.para: Function must be used in the blocking mode only. In case of non-blocking mode, an error event is generated. Function clears error sources after reading.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the driver instance structure.

briefdescription.para: Function for checking if there was new RX data since the last check.

detaileddescription.para: Function checks NRF_UARTE_EVENT_RXDRDY event and clears it if it was set.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the driver instance structure.
At least one byte was received since the last check.
No new data was received since the last check.

detaileddescription.para: The function enables the NRF_UARTE_EVENT_RXDRDY hardware event which is generated whenever a byte is received in RXD registers. The event indicates only that data is received, hence it must not be used yet because it may not be present yet in the RAM buffer handled by the EasyDMA. The event can be used only to detect a receiver activity. The event can be enabled at any time. Enabling it may increase the number of interrupts (after each received byte).

simplesect.para: If there were a receiver activity prior to enabling the NRF_UARTE_EVENT_RXDRDY event, the NRF_UARTE_EVENT_RXDRDY event may already be set and the NRFX_UARTE_EVT_RX_BYTE will be triggered immediately. To avoid that, it is recommended to clear that event by calling the nrfx_uarte_rx_new_data_check.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the driver instance structure.

detaileddescription.para: The function disables the RXDRDY hardware event. See the nrfx_uarte_rxdrdy_enable for more details. The event can be disabled at any time.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the driver instance structure.

detaileddescription.para: Function can be used to jump into UARTE interrupt context. User handler is called with the event NRFX_UARTE_EVT_TRIGGER.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the driver instance structure.
If interrupt is successfully triggered.
Failure. User handler is not configured.
Pointer to the driver instance structure.

briefdescription.para: Macro for creating a UARTE driver instance.

detaileddescription.para:
This configuration sets up UARTE with the following options:
hardware flow control disabled
no parity bit
one stop bit
baudrate: 115200

parameterdescription.para: TX pin.
RX pin.
